pipeline_health.notes: All 7 agents actively producing output today. No stalls detected. Pipeline is healthy and responsive.

workspace_freshness.assessment: No FUSE staleness detected this run. All key data files in sync.

handoff_issues.description: PERSISTENT (6th consecutive report): digest-reviews.js processedWinIds fallback at line 144 still masks Cycle 2 reviews for WINs whose Cycle 1 review was previously processed. 66 c2 review files on disk, 64 in processed ledger. 2 unprocessed: EXHIBIT-AB.c2.json and WIN-062.c2.json. The pending digest currently shows only 2 items (WIN-068, WIN-069) which are genuinely new WINs without c1 history — so the bug's natural mitigation continues to work for new WINs. BUT: WIN-062.c2.json is being masked by the legacy fallback (WIN-062.json was processed in Cycle 1). This means the decider never sees WIN-062's Cycle 2 review findings.
handoff_issues.evidence: digest-reviews.js line 144: processedWinIds.has(entry.win_id) treats WIN-062.c2.json as processed because WIN-062.json was in the ledger. WIN-062.c2.json written Apr 8 12:13, not in processed-reviews.json, not in pending digest.

suggested_fix.rationale: Remove both legacy fallbacks. The processedSet (which checks by exact filename) is the correct deduplication mechanism. The processedWinIds fallback was a transitional measure from when the ledger used bare WIN IDs — that migration is long complete. Removing it makes c2 reviews visible to the digest whenever they haven't been explicitly processed by filename.

handoff_issues.description: Decider still producing future-dated filenames. Files stamped 2026-04-09 were created on April 8. Not functionally broken but creates confusion during freshness audits.
handoff_issues.evidence: daily-report-2026-04-09T10-00.json and suggested-patches-2026-04-09T06-30.json both have filesystem mtime of Apr 8.

suggested_fix.rationale: The decider's timestamp generation likely uses a timezone offset. Low priority — the decider clones fresh and uses system time. This is a cosmetic issue in the decider's timestamp formatting logic, not a prompt bug.

prompt_issues.description: Tinker prompt at 501 lines — 100% over the 250-line ceiling. Grew 11 lines since last report (was 490). The tinker is now the 3rd largest prompt. Self-audit: this is my own prompt and I should be the first to fix it.

suggested_fix.rationale: Needs PROP-003: extract cost engineering section (steps 11a-11e, ~100 lines) and agent infrastructure audit (steps 8a-8d, ~80 lines) into reference files. Would bring core prompt to ~320 lines. See proposals.

prompt_issues.description: Analyst prompt at 436 lines — still 74% over ceiling. Decreased from 598 (PROP-001 partially applied or natural trim). Still needs dispatcher architecture per PROP-001.

suggested_fix.rationale: PROP-001 exists. Verify if partially applied. Core issue: 5 modes in one prompt means 80% of instructions are irrelevant on any given run.

prompt_issues.description: Decider prompt at 453 lines — 81% over ceiling. Decreased from 577 (PROP-002 partially applied or natural trim). Still far above target.

suggested_fix.rationale: PROP-002 exists. Verify if partially applied.

ISS-442 is a long-standing taxonomy question.

curmudgeon_health.tracker_cycle_field: current_cycle=2 (note: top-level 'cycle' key is undefined — tracker uses 'current_cycle' instead)
curmudgeon_health.assessment: Curmudgeon is making steady progress in Cycle 2. 4 WINs remaining before moving to sections. The tracker's 'cycle' field is undefined but 'current_cycle' is correctly set to 2 — this is a schema inconsistency, not a bug (the prompt and code reference 'current_cycle').

v6_restructure_audit.assessment: V6 restructure references are clean. No old-style section IDs detected in active pipeline files.

schedule_health.assessment: All agents running on schedule. No missed runs detected. Curmudgeon producing ~6-8 reviews per day in Cycle 2 (good throughput for 10-min intervals with Opus). Decider running multiple times per day and producing patches. Poller every 4h with dome site stable — appropriate frequency.

error_patterns.social_github_api: Social report from Apr 7 had -1 values for github_activity.forks, recent_commits, new_issues due to gh CLI unavailable. Apr 8 reports appear to use web search as fallback — no -1 values in run4. Mitigation is working.

error_patterns.digest_script_bug: Decider self-reported this bug in daily-report-2026-04-08T08-00.json and daily-report-2026-04-08T09-00.json. The decider is aware and working around it by manually checking c2 files. Structural fix still needed in digest-reviews.js.

recommendations.description: Fix digest-reviews.js line 144: remove processedWinIds legacy fallback. This is a mechanical code fix — replace the 2-line isProcessed check with filename-only check. 6th consecutive escalation.
recommendations.notes: This is in build-scripts/ which tinker cannot self-fix per rules. But the fix is trivial and well-defined. The decider has self-reported this bug twice.

recommendations.description: Continue prompt diet work. All 3 major prompts (analyst 436, decider 453, tinker 501) remain well above 250-line ceiling. PROP-001 and PROP-002 exist. PROP-003 needed for tinker self-diet.
ISS-442 (Misleading verdict overload) is the oldest open issue and requires human decision on whether to split the category. Flag for next human review session.

agent_efficiency.recommendation: Currently efficient — producing expansions (EXP-037/038/039) and fingerprints (WIN-065/066/067) every run. V51.1 changes gave it real work.
Efficient — reviewing 1-2 WINs per run consistently. Cycle 2 provides meaningful re-review work.
Mostly efficient. Some runs produce no patches when digest is empty. The digest bug means it sometimes sees fewer items than it should. Fixing the digest bug would improve this.
V51.1 dome changes gave it real work (run 1-2 found author activity). Runs 3-4 largely confirmed no new activity. Consider reducing to 2 runs/day or adding a skip-signal when dome site is stable.
V51.1 changes detected. When dome is stable, most runs confirm 'no change'. Acceptable overhead for the value of early detection.
